---
title: "Projects"
toc: false
---

<div class="project-grid">

<div class="card-item">
<span class="tag-badge">Bitcoin</span>

### Bitcoin Transaction

Bitcoin transactions can reveal behaviors relative to black market activities, currency crisis in certain countries, and more. We aim to detect patterns in the transaction graph and understand user behaviors.

<div class="meta">Python, R, SQLite, Neo4j · Dec 2020</div>

[Slides](files/slides/bitcoinslide/index.html){.link-btn target="_blank"}
[Draft Report](files/papers/bitcoin-paper.pdf){.link-btn target="_blank"}
[Gitbook](https://szuhannah.gitbook.io/bitcoin-address-clustering-with-the-famous){.link-btn target="_blank"}
[Code](https://github.com/SzuHannah/bitcoin-data-process){.link-btn target="_blank"}
</div>

<div class="card-item">
<span class="tag-badge">Microbiome</span>

### Environment, Soil pH, and Microbiome

Analyzed data from the Earth Microbiome Project, observing that soil bacteria diversity and function are influenced by different environments and soil pH. Used Vintage Sparse PCA (VSP).

<div class="meta">Authors: Emily Diaz Vallejo & Hannah Wang</div>

[Report](files/papers/Microbial_Report.pdf){.link-btn target="_blank"}
[Code](https://github.com/SzuHannah/Microbial-graph){.link-btn target="_blank"}
</div>

<div class="card-item">
<span class="tag-badge">Twitter</span>

### Murmuration

This project was condusted when I was a data analyst at Dr. Karl Rohe's Lab. The web application aims to visualize public attention across political and cultural flocks on Twitter.

[Website](https://murmuration.wisc.edu/){.link-btn target="_blank"}
</div>

<div class="card-item">
<span class="tag-badge">Twitter</span>

### Coronavirus Tweet

An R Shiny app that allows users to explore tweets under #coronavirus, leveraging VSP to cluster tweets by topic and flock.

<!-- <div class="meta">R Shiny</div> -->
[R shiny app](https://hannahwang.shinyapps.io/CoronavirusTweet/){.link-btn target="_blank"}
[Code](https://github.com/SzuHannah/CoronavirusTweet){.link-btn target="_blank"}
</div>

</div>
